summary: Workflow follows best practices (least-privilege permissions, pinned action refs, proper triggers, pwsh shell). PowerShell scripts use proper cmdlet naming and parameter validation. Main gaps: (1) run-tests.ps1 lines 75–105 repeat `-notmatch` assertions ~10 times with identical error-throwing logic—factor into helper. (2) Hardcoded assertions (expects exactly '14 tests', 'total=5 expired=2 warning=1 ok=2') are brittle; coupling test harness to fixture counts makes changes risky. (3) YAML validation uses regex (lines 49–57) instead of proper parser—fragile if workflow structure evolves. (4) Missing `Set-StrictMode` in main scripts. (5) Two validator steps in workflow (JSON/Markdown, lines 28–45) share boilerplate. Core validator logic is solid and modular; test harness functionality works but maintainability is compromised by hardcoded expectations and repetitive patterns.
